[2018-04-09 18:52] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Is "unit loses blocker when at 1 lifespan" too convoluted of a mechanic?
[2018-04-09 19:00] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Loonark has avoided any conditional cards, so I was wondering if we should as well (resonate cards with "for each" are similar, but slightly different)
[2018-04-09 19:02] wilson0862 (Wilson): What does that do other than effectively reduce a lifespan on units like dmech?
[2018-04-09 19:02] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Also naming cards is tough because I keep wanting to use phrases instead of concrete nouns.
[2018-04-09 19:03] wilson0862 (Wilson): I guess it reduces the lifespan basically but doesn't die to freeze. 🤔
[2018-04-09 19:04] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): It forces you to hold on 2 lifespan vs 2 clicks. Also enables shenanigans on lifespan blockers.  I'll post my unit soon for context
[2018-04-09 19:05] wilson0862 (Wilson): Cool. That'd probably help my dumb brain, haha
[2018-04-09 19:15] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Katamari Barricade (2 :Gold: :Green: :Green: , 10 Supply) 2 :Health: 
:Prompt:, :Blocker:, :Fragile: , :Lifespan: 2
:Click: , and pay 1 :Gold: :Green: : Gain +1 :Lifespan: and gain + 2 :Fragile: (maximum health of 10)
[2018-04-09 19:16] .bky_1556 (bky): What about "Has :Blocker: while :Prompt: "?
[2018-04-09 19:16] .bky_1556 (bky): Oh, you're also reversing lifespan.
[2018-04-09 19:17] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): It didn't need the conditional blocker, but I was wondering if that make it cleaner.
[2018-04-09 19:17] .bky_1556 (bky): "Has :Blocker: while :Prompt: " is a nice mechanic, though; I'm pocketing it.
[2018-04-09 19:17] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): SSP had that on a few of his units, with generally poor reception
[2018-04-09 19:19] darkeisbein: what is that even supposed to mean?
[2018-04-09 19:20] darkeisbein: like I can't figure out what that sentence is trying to tell me
[2018-04-09 19:20] darkeisbein: Has :Prompt: while :Blocker:
[2018-04-09 19:21] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Only blocks on first turn
[2018-04-09 19:21] .bky_1556 (bky): It needs to be the other way around.
[2018-04-09 19:21] darkeisbein: ok but what does it say if I do the color switcheroo?
[2018-04-09 19:21] darkeisbein: like a unit needs to be a blocker in order to be prompt
[2018-04-09 19:22] darkeisbein: so I don't see how it's supposed to work this was around
[2018-04-09 19:22] darkeisbein: the other way around makes no sense either though
[2018-04-09 19:24] darkeisbein: like if you want a unit that only absorbs for one turn make it like proto or feral warden
[2018-04-09 19:24] .bky_1556 (bky): Feral can be bought and decline to block until later.
[2018-04-09 19:27] darkeisbein: yes but in 90% of the cases feral does the same thing
[2018-04-09 19:27] darkeisbein: and it's much more elegant
[2018-04-09 19:30] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): And it has to be green, and it's breach vulnerable after blocking.  No doubt that it's more elegant, but not interchangeable.
[2018-04-09 19:34] darkeisbein: sure but why make an ugly design if it you could do a really similar thing that's not ugly

[2018-04-09 20:18] kaelii: Hrm. Resonance blocker, how to do how to do...
[2018-04-09 20:25] kaelii: Initial approach probably looks something like Nexus Grid: ( ? :Gold: ? :Blue: ? :Green: , supply 1) :Fragile: 1?
:Blocker: , ? :Time: 
On your turn, for each :Prompt: Gain 1 :Fragile: . (Max HP? Max per turn? Intended as counting a unit that has prompt on their card even if they are not currently prompt)
[2018-04-09 20:31] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): @Deleted User, I wait until it's 00AM then post up to 2 units before next 00AM.
[2018-04-09 20:34] darkeisbein: seems weird, like in base set the only thing this works with is mass forcefields
[2018-04-09 20:35] darkeisbein: but it has potentially crazy synergy with husk producing stuff
[2018-04-09 20:35] darkeisbein: I don't think there is any way to balance this that doesn't run into the issue of being very strong with husk producers
[2018-04-09 20:35] darkeisbein: without being useless in without them
[2018-04-09 20:36] darkeisbein: also being punished for trying to set up slow soak seems bad, slow absorb should be more efficient then prompt absorb because it takes effort to set up
[2018-04-09 20:37] kaelii: Yeah but if it works off blockers then everything from splitter to wall and rhino is potentially fair game.
[2018-04-09 20:37] kaelii: And I don't think it really needs to support odin or anything.
[2018-04-09 20:38] kaelii: If it gains HP based off of the HP of the prompt thing then bigger prompt things get husk-ish value and you almost certainly need to look into a cap on either HP or HP per turn so it isn't bought with centurion
[2018-04-09 20:39] kaelii: Also xaetron would be strange then.
[2018-04-09 20:41] kaelii: So it is at least very difficult to make  something gain health for having prompt/blockers.
[2018-04-09 20:41] kaelii: Unless it is like specifically getting bonuses from one type of base set unit -- say walls.
[2018-04-09 20:43] kaelii: But walls are clunky enough that if there is a niche for such a unit, it will be precarious because you want/need to use walls and really can't/don't want to over build them.

[2018-04-10 02:28] masn6811 (Masne98888): fission turret breachproof 🤔
[2018-04-10 02:28] .jrkirby (jrkirby): ```Even those themes are broken sometimes.```
[2018-04-10 02:28] masn6811 (Masne98888): that's because that's not a green theme
[2018-04-10 02:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): wild drone can 'absorb', is that a pie break? 🤔
[2018-04-10 02:29] .bky_1556 (bky): Energy units have their own theme - gold.
[2018-04-10 02:29] masn6811 (Masne98888): the best breachproof units are green, but that doesnt mean that green has to be reserved to good bp
[2018-04-10 02:30] masn6811 (Masne98888): just like how blue has the best absorb, but drake can be blue, even if it doesn't absorb
[2018-04-10 02:30] liadahlia (Lia): the best bp unit in the game is blue red :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-04-10 02:30] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Those are the themes as informed by the base set
[2018-04-10 02:31] mrguy888: color pie is useful when it opens up design space and bad when it is being used to restrict design space
[2018-04-10 02:31] .bky_1556 (bky): Most mechanical themes have one good tech, one secondary tech and one worst tech.
[2018-04-10 02:31] http404error: Wild Drone can't absorb. What do you mean by that?
[2018-04-10 02:31] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Expect a new Mechanic for tomorrow.
[2018-04-10 02:31] .bky_1556 (bky): Like blue gets best absorb, red gets a little absorb and green only gets fake absorb.
[2018-04-10 02:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): wild drone absorbs when it's the only unit they have left :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-04-10 02:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): the same is not true for say, conduit
[2018-04-10 02:32] .bky_1556 (bky): Red gets best prompt granularity, green gets exploitable prompt granularity (FF or Barrier) and blue gets no prompt granularity better than wall.
[2018-04-10 02:32] masn6811 (Masne98888): color pies are meant to restrict design space. these restrictions are nessasary, or else you'll have red absorbers flying around
[2018-04-10 02:33] .bky_1556 (bky): And with breachproof, G is most breachproof, B gets medium-breachproof and red is all breach vulnerable.
[2018-04-10 02:33] liadahlia (Lia): i think it's good to try and stick to the color pie when possible, but it doesn't *have* to be
[2018-04-10 02:35] masn6811 (Masne98888): the reason for color pie is so that we can have balanced sets. we don't want games where the best attack, the best absorb, and the best soak is red
[2018-04-10 02:35] masn6811 (Masne98888): (sometimes we end up with games where best attack, absorb, and soak are blue) :monkaS:
[2018-04-10 02:37] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I agree with you that the color themes are good, I just think that my design was a relatively minor infraction
[2018-04-10 02:39] liadahlia (Lia): another reason to have thematically colored units is that it acts as a mental shortcut. "oh a new blue unit, is probably durable and good at absorbing"
[2018-04-10 02:39] .bky_1556 (bky): That's why explodey wall had to be BG rather than just B; mono-B doesn't typically get 2 HP prompt because of granularity concerns.
[2018-04-10 02:39] liadahlia (Lia): and also too, it's not just individual units that needs to have this characteristic, the fact that wall uses blue means that any blue units are better at defense due to the fact that you can buy walls along with that unit
[2018-04-10 02:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, i proposed "Panel" a while back, which is just a 1B husk
[2018-04-10 02:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): but granularity concerns is a thing
[2018-04-10 02:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): panel being to pixie as barrier is to gauss charge...
[2018-04-10 02:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): but if pixie and barrier are fine, while gauss charge is weak, then panel is too stronk
[2018-04-10 02:41] .jrkirby (jrkirby): "Explodey Wall"? That's not BG, that's 7RRGG
[2018-04-10 02:42] .bky_1556 (bky): Panel would need to be frontline.
[2018-04-10 02:42] liadahlia (Lia): i mean i feel like panel is a pretty reasonable unit, 2.66 barrier value, worse than walls, but you gain granularity and the ability to blue sink
[2018-04-10 02:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, i was thinking that barrier should have :Frontline: as opposed to :Lifespan: 1
[2018-04-10 02:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): but apperently the devs dont agree
[2018-04-10 02:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): i still think it should be that way
[2018-04-10 02:43] liadahlia (Lia): it's just a different flavor of being exploited
[2018-04-10 02:43] liadahlia (Lia): isn't it easier to exploit frontline than absorb
[2018-04-10 02:43] liadahlia (Lia): which would make barrier worse than it is
[2018-04-10 02:43] .jrkirby (jrkirby): if barrier had :Frontline: instead of :Lifespan: .... man that'd be so dang strong
[2018-04-10 02:44] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): I.E: Blue: 7BB. tX3 f2 pay GR get FT.
[2018-04-10 02:45] liadahlia (Lia): it'd be stronger against chill, but it'd be weaker against granular attacks
